channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Camp Pendleton identifies Marine killed in Philippines surveillance mission
item.description: Marine Corps officials at Camp Pendleton on Sunday identified Sgt. Jacob M. Durham as the U.S. Marine who tragically lost his life in an aircraft incident on Feb. 6, 2025, in the Philippine Province of Maguindanao del Sur.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles wildfire czar's $500K paycheck for 90 days of work draws swift blowback, mayor reverses course
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass reneged wildfire czar Steve Soboroff's $500,000 compensation for 90 days of work after the amount drew criticism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hollywood producer convicted of murder in deaths of model and her friend
item.description: A Beverly Hills man has been convicted of two murders and numerous sexual assaults after investigations into the deaths revealed a shocking past.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ivy League grad student, US Army veteran's killer had eerie motive: detective
item.description: A Yale graduate student and U.S. army veteran was killed in what initially appeared to be a random act of violence, and a detective on the case reveals how the killer was nabbed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ecuador votes for president, with the conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer in the lead
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador’s presidential election Sunday is shaping up to be a repeat of the 2023 race, when voters chose a young, conservative millionaire over the leftist protégée of the country’s most influential president this century.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump official orders consumer protection agency to stop work
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US officials are bound for Europe for top-level talks on Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump’s national security adviser said Sunday that top administration officials will meet with European officials this week about how to end the war in Ukraine, nearly three years after Russia launched an all-out invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US Marine from California identified as one of 4 people killed in Philippines plane crash
item.description: A U.S. Marine from California has been identified by military officials as one of four people killed when a plane contracted by the U.S. military crashed in a rice field in the southern Philippines.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Popular donut products recalled over listeria danger
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes. The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) issued a report on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, KTLA 5

item.title: How Republican skeptics in the Senate got to 'yes' on RFK Jr. and Gabbard
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican skepticism in the Senate of President Donald Trump’sCabinet nominees has been worn down, putting his unconventional choices for some of the most powerful positions in the federal government on the verge of confirmation. Floor votes are expected this week on Robert F.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: ICE arrests illegal migrant accused of entering the country 6 times
item.description: Jorge Carvajal Castrejon, 36, an illegal migrant, was arrested Jan. 28 in Houston after allegedly entering the US six times, during an enhanced ICE operation with partners from the FBI and other agencies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unusual video from Florida police shows note on 'first date' brick woman allegedly hurled through ex's window
item.description: Local authorities in Florida release an unusual video of a woman throwing a brick through her ex-boyfriend's window after she initially denied the act and was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What makes a dog a winner? A Westminster Kennel Club judge explains
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The competitors can come in forms as different as a Chihuahua, a great Dane, a bulldog and a greyhound. Each one is a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nelly delivers hits at 'Homecoming' Super Bowl week concert in historic New Orleans restaurant
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Nelly brought his signature St. Louis swagger to a New Orleans institution, turning a nearly seven-decade-old restaurant into a Super Bowl week party. The multi-Grammy winner packed Brennan’s on Friday night, delivering a hit-filled set at the h.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 12 states to sue over DOGE access to payment systems containing personal data
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law," a statement from the Democratic attorneys general reads.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Recall for broccoli sold at Walmart in 20 states upgraded to highest risk level: FDA
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has upgraded its classification of a broccoli recall initially reported in late December, labeling it as “Class I” — the agency’s most serious recall category.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tips to easily cancel your subscriptions
item.description: The FTC says that it received about 70 consumer complaints a day in 2024 about recurring subscriptions, up from 42 per day in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: LeBron James will sit out the Lakers' game against Indiana to rest his sore left ankle
item.description: LeBron James is sitting out the Los Angeles Lakers ' game against Indiana on Saturday to rest his sore left ankle.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, ABC 4

item.title: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron dies at 74
item.description: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron, who led the Chicago Bears to the playoffs and was voted AP coach of the year in 2001, died Saturday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family files lawsuit after mother, son die from food poisoning while on vacation
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FDA recalls 2 million donut products over listeria danger
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, NewsNation

item.title: 2 million donut products recalled nationwide over listeria risk: FDA
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Generations later, a remedy to destroying Black neighborhoods is fulfilled in Michigan
item.description: HAMTRAMCK, Mich. (AP) — Leslie Knox was a young girl in the 1960s when her Detroit-area city was accused of destroying neighborhoods to get rid of Black residents.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: Starbucks offering free coffee Monday after the Super Bowl: How to get yours
item.description: Starbucks is offering a free pick-me-up on Monday for football fans who might be staying up late celebrating (or fuming over) the results of the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Jane Alvarez-Wertz, Fox 5

item.title: Fetterman says he'll vote against RFK Jr., Gabbard nominations
item.description: "I have voted against moving forward to the confirmation of Ms. Gabbard and Mr. Kennedy, and will be voting NO on their confirmations.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, KRON4

item.title: Protesters in cities across the US rally against Trump's policies, Project 2025 and Elon Musk
item.description: Demonstrators gathered in cities across the U.S. on Wednesday to protest the Trump administration's early actions, decrying everything from the president's immigration crackdown to his rollback of transgender rights and a proposal to forcibly transfer Palestinians from the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4
